rationale: Voice follow-up liveness set (QA-EVAL-R2.12 / QA-EVAL-R11): deterministic checks over the projection/voice surface (no live engine call). EXPAND climbs the verbosity ladder so the NEXT answer is strictly longer (more foregrounded numbers admitted within the per-length cap), NEVER shorter. DRILL / REVEAL_NUMBERS surface the requested ALREADY-grounded canonical number VERBATIM on the SAME durable thread WITHOUT widening scope (no new athlete_id, no new capability, no new citation record beyond the targeted observation's grounded numbers). A MONOTONE case asserts length-monotonicity: short <= standard <= detailed in the foregrounded-number budget. Each case is checked by the deterministic CODE (COACH-R8 ladder + reveal-verbatim), never the LLM (EVAL-R5). Negative cases drive the teeth.

cases.rationale: An EXPAND follow-up from a short answer climbs one rung up the ladder to standard (VOICE-R8 / COACH-R8) so the next answer admits more foregrounded numbers (cap 2 -> 3), i.e. is longer; it MUST NOT drop a rung.
EXPAND from standard climbs to detailed (cap 3 -> 4); strictly longer budget.
EXPAND at the top rung saturates at detailed (cap stays 4) — never wraps back down; the budget does not shrink, so it is still NOT shorter.

observations.text: Your form is a touch negative.

cases.rationale: A REVEAL_NUMBERS follow-up targeting obs-ctl surfaces the ALREADY-grounded canonical ctl=72.4 VERBATIM (VOICE-R9 / GROUND-R7) on the SAME durable thread; the authenticated identity and scope are UNCHANGED before/after (no scope-widen). The revealed citation is one already grounded on the thread, never a new claim.
A DRILL follow-up reveals the verbatim grounded critical_power=268.0 on the same thread without widening scope.
Length-monotonicity (VOICE-R7): the per-length foregrounded-number budget is monotone non-decreasing short <= standard <= detailed, so a longer response NEVER admits fewer numbers than a shorter one.

cases.question: How much training load have I done over the last six weeks?
cases.grounded_text: Hey there! Here is your current training-load picture from the latest daily metrics: your fitness (ctl) is 6.7, your fatigue (atl) is 30.2, and your form (tsb) is -28. These numbers show your fitness (ctl) is low, your fatigue (atl) is high, and your form (tsb) is deeply negative.
cases.rationale: EVAL-R5b deterministic ANSWER-voice gate: this is the exact cited-metrics-report the engine USED to ship (report-frame lead 'Here is your current training-load picture' + raw ctl/atl/tsb tokens + 3 foregrounded numbers). Driven through the real answer_question projection (with the config-loaded presentation policy) the DELIVERED answer MUST now lead with a state read (COACH-R7), contain NO raw internal metric token (VOICE-R2), foreground <= the standard cap of numbers (VOICE-R7), and every surfaced number MUST stay canonical (GROUND-R7) — the grounded citations are untouched.

negative_cases.question: How much training load have I done over the last six weeks?
negative_cases.grounded_text:
Here is your current training-load picture from the latest daily metrics:
- ctl: 6.7
- atl: 30.2
- tsb: -28
These numbers show your fitness is low.
negative_cases.rationale: Teeth (mutation-proof): the RAW old metric-report draft — a report-frame lead + a bare ctl/atl/tsb token list — asserted DIRECTLY (bypassing the presentation enforcement) MUST be flagged by the deterministic answer-voice check (report-frame lead AND raw metric tokens). If the enforcement were removed from answer_question this is exactly what would ship, so the check must FAIL it; a check that passes this raw draft is vacuous.
Teeth: a planted regression that DROPPED an EXPAND to a shorter rung (detailed -> short) violates the ladder; the deterministic check MUST flag the shorter result rather than passing it.
Teeth: a reveal that WIDENED scope (added export_all_athletes) MUST be flagged — a follow-up never widens scope (COACH-R8 / INJECT-R3).
Teeth: a reveal that surfaced a number (999.0) NOT matching the grounded citation (72.4) MUST be flagged — the reveal is verbatim off the prior grounding, never a new/fabricated value (GROUND-R7).

Teeth: a follow-up whose durable thread is bound to a DIFFERENT athlete (athlete-eval-9999) than the authenticated one (athlete-eval-0001) is a cross-identity thread and MUST be flagged — a follow-up resumes the SAME athlete's thread, never another's (AGT-SEC-R1 / CKPT-R3).
